task_text:
Fix missing CLI validation that `--shard-index` is less than `--shard-count`

In `internal/catch_commandline.cpp`, the `setShardIndex` handler validates only that the value is a non-negative integer but does not check that it is less than the configured shard count. When a user passes `--shard-index N` where `N >= --shard-count`, the `createShard` function in `internal/catch_sharding.hpp` is called with `shardIndex >= shardCount`, triggering `assert(shardCount > shardIndex)` in debug builds and undefined behaviour in release builds. Add a post-parse validation step (after both options are parsed) that returns a `runtimeError` when `shardIndex >= shardCount`.
solve_notes: The fix is placed in `Session::applyCommandLine` in `catch_session.cpp`, immediately after the CLI parse succeeds and the help/identify fast-paths run. This is the natural post-parse validation point: both `--shard-index` and `--shard-count` are already committed to `m_configData` when this code runs, regardless of their order on the command line. Replicates the existing error-display pattern (red error header, TextFlow column indent, `Run with -? for usage` trailer). The `ConfigData` defaults are `shardCount=1, shardIndex=0`, so the guard only fires when an explicit out-of-range combination is passed. Adding validation inside the per-option lambdas in `catch_commandline.cpp` would cause false positives when `--shard-index` appears before `--shard-count` on the command line, because the count has not been updated yet at that point.

search_sequence.result: `setShardIndex` at line 209 sets `config.shardIndex` with only an integer-parse guard; `setShardCount` at line 182 validates > 0; no cross-validation between the two
search_sequence.reasoning: Task description pointed to catch_commandline.cpp as the problem site

search_sequence.action: Inspected Clara parser for a post-parse validate hook
search_sequence.result: Clara has a `validate()` method on Arg/Opt objects, but it is called at parse-start per-token, not after all tokens; `Parser::parse()` has no user-defined post-parse callback (`// !TBD Check missing required options` comment confirms this is unimplemented)
search_sequence.reasoning: Needed to determine if a post-parse hook could be added inside buildCli without modifying session.cpp

search_sequence.result: After `m_cli.parse()` succeeds, control falls through show-help/libIdentify checks to `m_config.reset(); return 0;` — this is the natural post-parse validation site; both config fields are fully populated here
search_sequence.reasoning: Confirmed that session.cpp is the cleanest place for post-parse validation and that both shardIndex and shardCount are available

search_sequence.result: `shardCount = 1`, `shardIndex = 0` — default combination is valid; guard `shardIndex >= shardCount` fires only on explicit invalid combinations
search_sequence.reasoning: Needed to verify that the guard does not fire for the common case where neither shard option is specified

dead_ends.path: Adding the check inside setShardIndex in catch_commandline.cpp
dead_ends.reason: Would produce false positives when --shard-index appears before --shard-count because shardCount is still at its default of 1 at the time setShardIndex runs

key_decisions.decision: Put the validation in `Session::applyCommandLine` rather than in the setShardIndex/setShardCount lambdas
alternatives: Check in both lambdas cross-validating against each other
key_decisions.reasoning: Session::applyCommandLine is called after all arguments are parsed, making it the only place where both shardCount and shardIndex are fully settled regardless of argument order

key_decisions.decision: Replicate the existing error display pattern (colour, TextFlow column, Run with -? footer)
key_decisions.reasoning: Consistency with the existing error handling in applyCommandLine ensures the error message looks identical to any other parse error

exploration_log.aha_moment: The `// !TBD Check missing required options` comment in Clara's `Parser::parse()` confirmed that there is no post-parse hook in Clara, pointing directly to session.cpp as the fix location.
exploration_log.hindsight: Could have started with session.cpp rather than trying to fit the fix into catch_commandline.cpp lambdas, saving time spent investigating Clara's validate() mechanism.

minimum_sufficient_defs.name: Session::applyCommandLine
minimum_sufficient_defs.reason: edited: added post-parse shardIndex >= shardCount guard after successful CLI parse; this is where both shard config values are fully settled

minimum_sufficient_defs.name: ConfigData
minimum_sufficient_defs.reason: read: needed to confirm shardCount default is 1 and shardIndex default is 0 so the guard fires only on explicit invalid combinations, not on default configuration

thrash_preventing_defs.name: setShardIndex
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: shows the lambda that sets config.shardIndex; confirms no cross-validation with shardCount and that argument order means shardCount may not be set yet when setShardIndex runs

thrash_preventing_defs.name: setShardCount
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: shows the pattern for error handling with ParserResult::runtimeError; also confirms shardCount>0 validation exists but no upper-bound check on shardIndex

thrash_preventing_defs.name: createShard
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: confirms `assert(shardCount > shardIndex)` that triggers on invalid input, motivating the validation addition

tier_difference_reasoning: minimum_sufficient is the edited method and ConfigData (for default values). thrash_preventing adds the two setShardX lambdas (to understand why session.cpp rather than commandline.cpp is the fix site) and createShard (to see the failing assert that the fix prevents). Without these, an agent might add validation inside the lambdas and introduce argument-order bugs.

excluded_defs.name: Parser
excluded_defs.reason: Investigated but excluded: Clara's validate() is per-token, not post-parse; the fact that it cannot be used for post-parse logic was a dead end

queries.query_text: Command line parser does not validate that shard index is less than shard count so the program crashes or triggers undefined behaviour when an out-of-range shard index is provided
queries.justification: Domain terms 'command line parser', 'shard index', 'shard count', 'undefined behaviour'. No symbol names. Pre-implementation framing of the assertion failure observed at runtime.
The literal lambda name `setShardIndex` is where the missing validation lives. Pre-implementation: a developer searching for the shard-index handling would search for this lambda name.

queries.query_text: setShardIndex, setShardCount, shardIndex, shardCount, applyCommandLine, ConfigData
queries.justification: Six exact symbol names relevant to the fix. Pre-implementation: a developer tracing the CLI shard handling would identify these names.

queries.query_text: methods of Session that perform post-parse validation of ConfigData fields
queries.justification: Uses class name 'Session' and concept 'post-parse validation'. Pre-implementation: a developer looking for where to add validation after CLI parsing would ask for Session methods.
Two explicit file paths plus the relevant symbol names. Pre-implementation: a developer directed to the shard CLI code would navigate to these files.

queries.query_text: Validate after CLI parse in Session::applyCommandLine that shardIndex is less than shardCount and return error if not
queries.justification: Mixes domain concept 'validate after CLI parse' with exact symbols. Pre-implementation framing after identifying the fix location.

queries.query_text: applyCommandLine shardIndex shardCount ConfigData in catch_session.cpp catch_config.hpp
queries.justification: Symbol names plus file paths. Pre-implementation: a developer who has located the session and config files would search for these symbols.

queries.query_text: In catch_session.cpp Session::applyCommandLine after CLI parse succeeds add guard if m_configData.shardIndex >= m_configData.shardCount return UnspecifiedErrorExitCode with error message
queries.justification: Includes file, class, method, field names, and the conditional logic. Pre-implementation framing after full root-cause analysis.

test_selection.test_query: Find tests that exercise Session::applyCommandLine or the sharding CLI flag parsing in catch_commandline.cpp

relevant_preexisting_tests.reason: Tests the shard CLI options via makeCommandLineParser(); exercises the setShardIndex and setShardCount lambdas whose interaction the fix addresses; the adjacent test patterns (error reporting for invalid values) model what the new validation should produce
